channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Socceroos' World Cup Journey: Heartbreak and Hope for Future Success
item.description: The Socceroos' World Cup campaign ended in heartbreak after a penalty shootout loss to Egypt, but their young squad and diverse team composition signal promising future prospects. Despite falling shor...

item.title: Tanzania's Dumisha Amani Project Empowers Youth Through Vocational Training and Entrepreneurship
item.description: The Dumisha Amani Project, funded by the United Nations Peace and Development Fund (UNPDF), United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Tanzania, and the Centre for Peace and Prosperity (CPP), is prov...

item.title: Political analyst criticizes the organization and execution of Trump's America 250 Independence Day celebration
item.description: A political analyst criticized President Donald Trump's July 4 celebration marking the 250th anniversary of American independence, arguing that the event failed to meet the high expectations promoted ...

item.title: Hunter voters back parts of One Nation's agenda but remain divided over Pauline Hanson's monoculturalism comments
item.description: SBS News visited communities in the NSW electorate of Hunter to examine growing support for One Nation and leader Pauline Hanson while exploring public reactions to her recent comments calling for Aus...

item.title: Federal IT outsourcing costs surge despite efforts to reduce contractor reliance
item.description: Federal spending on IT outsourcing has surged to $808 million, tripling over three years, despite policies aimed at reducing contractor reliance. A Canberra Times analysis of AusTender data reveals IT...

item.title: Sandile Ndlovu Recognized for Capturing Resilience and Hope Through Photography
item.description: Photographer Sandile Ndlovu has been honored as the Standard Bank Sikuvile Journalist of the Year 2026 for his impactful photo essays that highlight resilience, hope, and the lives of ordinary South A...

item.title: Jannik Sinner Advances to Wimbledon Quarterfinals and Focuses on Continued Improvement
item.description: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner continued his impressive Wimbledon campaign by defeating Japan’s Shintaro Mochizuki in straight sets, 6-3, 7-6(6), 6-3, to reach the quarterfinals for the fifth consecutive y...

item.title: FIFA faces criticism after suspending Folarin Balogun's World Cup ban ahead of Belgium match
item.description: The article argues that FIFA's decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension ahead of the United States' World Cup last-16 fixture against Belgium has raised serious concerns about sporti...

item.title: Brazil and Norway Clash in World Cup Last-16 Match as Key Players Vie for Progress
item.description: Brazil and Norway faced off in a high-stakes World Cup last-16 clash, with both teams showcasing formidable attacking talent. The match, held at the Estadio Azteca, saw Brazil's Bruno Guimaraes miss a...

item.title: Kaduna Governor Uba Sani Distributes Compensation to 122 Households Affected by Ring Road Project
item.description: Kaduna State Governor Uba Sani has reaffirmed that his administration will not allow the demolition of any citizen's property without paying fair and adequate compensation. He made this commitment dur...

item.title: China releases Pastor Ezra Jin after diplomatic intervention during Trump-Xi meeting
item.description: Chinese authorities have released Pastor Ezra Jin, founder of the underground Zion Church, after he spent months in detention following a major crackdown on unregistered Christian groups. Jin was arre...

item.title: Brazil and Norway Clash in FIFA World Cup Round of 16
item.description: The Round of 16 match between Brazil and Norway at the FIFA World Cup 2026 is underway at the New York New Jersey stadium in East Rutherford. Brazil, despite being favorites, has a historical record o...

item.title: Novak Djokovic breaks Federer's Wimbledon match-win record with victory over Safiullin
item.description: Novak Djokovic secured his 106th men's singles match win at Wimbledon, surpassing Roger Federer's record, after a hard-fought victory over Russian qualifier Roman Safiullin in the fourth round. The 39...

item.title: Simba Celebrates Domestic Cup Victory with Citywide Parade
item.description: Simba Football Club's victory parade in Dar es Salaam marked the end of a five-year trophy drought, with thousands of supporters transforming the city into a sea of red and white. The parade, followin...

item.title: Amaravati Journalists Demand Immediate Action on Pending Housing Project
item.description: Journalists from various media outlets in Andhra Pradesh have appealed to Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu to expedite the Amaravati housing project for journalists, which has been delayed for year...

item.title: Heat Advisory Issued for Los Angeles County and Southern California Amid Rising Temperatures
item.description: The National Weather Service has issued a heat advisory for Los Angeles County and other parts of Southern California, warning of elevated temperatures and fire weather conditions through Thursday. Me...

item.title: Plateau state tackles human trafficking crisis with victim support
item.description: The Plateau State Government has expressed deep concern over the alarming rise in human trafficking cases, following the rescue of three young women trafficked to Mali and forced into prostitution. Th...

item.title: More Than 400 Arrested After Fourth of July Disturbances in Newport Beach
item.description: Newport Beach officials reported that more than 400 people, many of them young visitors and minors, were arrested during a chaotic Fourth of July weekend that featured illegal fireworks, street fights...

item.title: Catholic Group Excommunicated by Pope Leo Holds Mass
item.description: A rebel Catholic group that was excommunicated by Pope Leo recently celebrated mass, sparking discussions about church discipline and doctrinal adherence. The incident involves the Society of St. Pius...

item.title: Pat Utomi Condemns Alleged Abuse of Presidential Authority and Repeats Call for Age Limit in Nigerian Politics
item.description: Former presidential candidate Pat Utomi has alleged that impunity and disregard for due process have become deeply rooted in Nigeria's political system. In a post shared on his X account, Utomi argued...
